successor: CA Inc. (2006) -> CA Technologies (2010) -> Broadcom acquired 2018
notes: Founded 1976 by Charles B. Wang; third-largest independent software company by 1997; revenues $4B+ with Unicenter at ~$1B run rate. Renamed CA Inc. (2006) then CA Technologies (2010). Acquired by Broadcom Inc. for $18.9B in 2018.
IBM acquired Tivoli Systems 1996; developed Tivoli Management Environment (TME) as enterprise management platform competing directly with Unicenter TNG.

HP OpenView named as 'serious competitor' with large installed base; Aberdeen states HP 'has clearly lost momentum'; HP's systems division nonetheless endorsed Unicenter Framework as preferred management platform.
Solstice enterprise management product; Aberdeen notes Sun 'more interested in partnering for future technology than building own new products.'
Named as ISV partner CA was 'actively lining up' to use Framework as application management base; Unicenter had SAP R/3 management in optional advanced options.
Named as example of competitor that struggled; 'after a difficult start-up phase, OpenVision was sold earlier this year' (1997). Acquired by Veritas Software.
CA acquired Digital Equipment's Polycenter management products; DEC's systems division listed as Framework hardware partner.
Author Peter S. Kastner ('PK' in filename) was Aberdeen Group's senior analyst covering enterprise management software; Aberdeen installed the Framework in 10 minutes on its own NT server.
